functions: Forkhead box (FOX) transcription factor that shapes chromatin accessibility and defines senescence-specific enhancer landscapes
Stimulates H3K27 acetylation and chromatin opening at de novo enhancers of pro-inflammatory SASP genes
Mesenchymal transcription factor driving epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) transcriptional signatures
Promotes myeloid differentiation while suppressing T-cell differentiation in CD34+ hematopoietic progenitors
Transcriptional activator of EZH2, mediating epigenetic silencing of DKK3 via H3K27me3
Upregulates antioxidant proteins FDX1 and HO-1, reducing labile iron pool and ROS
Activates viral chromatin by acetylation to promote alphaherpesvirus (HSV-1) lytic replication and reactivation from latency
Lung endothelial-specific transcription factor binding the S1PR1 promoter to regulate its expression

disease_associations.disease: T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia / mixed phenotype acute leukemia

cellular_states: Lung endothelial cells / brain microvascular endothelial cells (BBB)
pubmed.druggability_notes: FOXF1-mediated enhancer remodeling proposed as a target for modulating senescent cells to promote healthy aging. FOXF1/FENDRR B-ALL subtype may benefit from early immunotherapy (blinatumomab) or targeted interventions. Identified as a potential therapeutic target in pulmonary fibrosis (iron/ROS-FOXF1 feedback loop), renal fibrosis (FOXF1-EZH2-DKK3 axis), and as anti-pneumonia genetic resource. Acts via epigenetic cofactors (p300/CBP) at viral and host chromatin.

functions: DNA-binding transcription activator (RNA polymerase II-specific)
Probable transcription activator for lung-specific genes
Sequence-specific RNA polymerase II cis-regulatory region DNA binding
Positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II
Regulation of lung development and lung vasculature development
Regulation of blood vessel and venous blood vessel development
Regulation of heart development and cardiac left ventricle morphogenesis
Regulation of digestive tract, pancreas, trachea, and ureter development
cellular_states: Developing embryonic tissues (heart, digestive tract, pancreas, trachea, ureter)
uniprot.druggability_notes: No druggability, binding pocket, or drug class information is provided in the text. As a nuclear DNA-binding transcription factor, no druggability features are described.

functions: Probable transcription activator for a number of lung-specific genes
Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor (forkhead box family)
Regulates CDH11 gene transcription
Involved in lateral plate mesoderm formation

disease_associations.disease: Alveolar capillary dysplasia with misalignment of pulmonary veins
